title: Product on the segment by modulo

content.problemStatement: Given an array ** A ** of ** N ** integers and an integer ** P ** ( not necessarily prime ). You are required to answer ** Q ** queries, **i**-th query is described by two numbers **Li, Ri**, the answer to the query is the product of all numbers in array A from index **Li** to index Ri modulo **P**. You have to answer the queries in online mode, that is you can't know a query before you answer all queries that are before it.
constraints: The sum of **N** over all testcases does not exceed 106 and sum of **Q** over all testcases does not exceed 2 * 107
**Subtask 1** (20 points) : **P** is prime and all numbers of A in range [1; **P** - 1]
**Subtask 1** (20 points) : **P = **xk**** where x is prime and k is integer   and all numbers of A in range [1; **P** - 1]
content.inputFormat:
the first line contains one integer ** T **, the number of test-cases.

the first line of each test-case contains ** N, P, Q **.

the third line contains **N** space-separated integers, the of elements array ** A **.

the fourth line contains array **B** which consists of floor(Q/64) + 2 integers . Let's number the queries starting from 0. If **i** is divisible by 64 then: 


** Li = (Bi / 64 + x) mod N, Ri = (Bi / 64 + 1 + x) mod n **.

Otherwise: ** Li = (Li-1 + x) mod N, Ri =  (Ri-1 + x) mod N **. If ** Li > Ri ** you should swap them. here x is equal to 0 if it's the first query, otherwise it's equal to the answer of the previous query plus 1 modulo **P**.
content.outputFormat: After each test-case you have to print one number - the value of x after the the last query modulo P
